questions.question: What is the primary lossy step in the JPEG pipeline?
questions.explanation: Quantization divides DCT coefficients by the quantization table, discarding high-frequency detail. All other steps are lossless or reversible.

questions.question: Why does JPEG convert RGB to YCbCr before compressing?
options: It separates luminance from chrominance so chroma can be subsampled without much perceived loss
YCbCr is required by the DCT
questions.explanation: The eye is more sensitive to luminance detail than color detail. Separating Y from Cb/Cr allows 4:2:0 chroma subsampling, cutting data significantly.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the zigzag scan in JPEG?
options: To reorder coefficients from low to high frequency, grouping trailing zeros for run-length encoding
questions.explanation: Zigzag scan traverses the 8x8 block from low-frequency to high-frequency coefficients, concentrating zeros at the end for efficient run-length encoding.

questions.question: Why is JPEG poorly suited for line art or text images?
options: JPEG cannot handle black and white
Sharp edges contain high-frequency energy that quantization discards, causing ringing artifacts
questions.explanation: Line art and text have sharp high-frequency edges. The quantization table aggressively reduces high-frequency coefficients, producing visible ringing and blurring.
